frame_analyses.description: Trainer at rest state. Six rubber objects visible: 2 green on left pegs, 2 orange on right pegs, 1 orange in center bowl, 1 gray peg at top center. Two laparoscopic graspers visible at instrument ports.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Initial setup ready. Blue ball visible for task activation.

frame_analyses.description: Trainee engaging with blue ball activation mechanism; grasper approaching central ball at timestamp 00:00.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Appropriate approach to activate task start.

frame_analyses.description: Both instruments now active. Left grasper (non-dominant hand) beginning to grasp orange object from center bowl.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Left instrument initiating first transfer; smooth approach to center object.

frame_analyses.description: Left grasper lifting orange marble from center bowl, moving toward left side pegs. Right grasper idle.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clean vertical lift, object appears secure in grasper.

frame_analyses.description: Left grasper depositing orange object onto top-left peg. Object visibly placed on peg surface.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Controlled placement, object seated on peg.

frame_analyses.description: Left grasper now grasping second orange object from center bowl. Right grasper beginning to approach.
Left grasper transferring second orange object across field. Right grasper positioning to retrieve green marble from left peg.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Bimanual coordination evident; parallel transfers beginning.

frame_analyses.description: Left grasper placing second orange object on lower-left peg. Right grasper grasping green marble from left peg.
Right grasper holding green marble (transferred from left peg). Left grasper retrieving second green marble from left lower peg.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Reverse transfer phase initiated. Both instruments engaged.

frame_analyses.description: Right grasper carrying green marble toward right-side peg. Left grasper carrying second green marble in parallel transfer.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Smooth bimanual midair transfer; objects appear stable.

frame_analyses.description: Right grasper placing green marble on right-side peg. Second green marble being placed on lower-right peg simultaneously by left instrument.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Coordinated bilateral placement. Objects seating properly on pegs.

frame_analyses.description: Final two orange marbles being transferred from center bowl to right-side pegs. Left grasper placing final object. All 6 objects now distributed: 2 green on right pegs, 4 orange (2 on left, 2 on right).
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Efficient completion. Smooth grasper management throughout.

frame_analyses.description: Task completion confirmed at 00:48. All 6 objects successfully transferred. Display shows 'Exercise completed.' Instruments at rest.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Complete task accomplished within time limits. No objects dropped or remain in field.

penalties.description: No objects dropped outside field or during transfer
All 6 objects successfully placed on designated pegs; none remain in center bowl
Task completed at 48 seconds, well within 300-second limit

bimanual_coordination.notes: Trainee demonstrated smooth bimanual coordination with minimal instrument interference

confidence_rationale: Completion time clearly visible at 00:48 on screen. All 6 objects visibly transferred to correct pegs with no drops or remaining objects. Task completion screen confirms successful execution. Only minor uncertainty relates to exact frame-by-frame phase transitions, but overall performance metrics are unambiguous.
technique_summary: Excellent performance on Peg Transfer task. Trainee completed all 6 object transfers in 48 seconds with zero penalties. Demonstrated expert-level bimanual coordination, smooth instrument control, and efficient movement patterns. Time places performance in expert range (40-60s benchmark). No objects dropped, no procedural errors, no timeout issues.
strengths: Rapid completion time (48s) places trainee in expert performance bracket
Flawless object placement—all 6 objects successfully transferred without drops
Smooth bimanual coordination with minimal instrument collision or hesitation
Efficient reverse transfer phase showing good hand switching discipline
Clean grasper mechanics throughout all phases
improvement_suggestions: Already performing at expert level; further refinement would focus on sub-40s completion times through optimization of approach and retraction movements
